_comment: Measured on the M1 (single core, 60 s). Every score is canonically rescored from the engine's own board output (community/canonical_rescore.py, validated to reproduce the known 469 board); engine self-reports are not trusted. Throughput is per-engine and never cross-compared. Rerun with community/run_community.sh then community/build_site_data.py.

engines.name: mcgavin-c
engines.note: Codegen strict/break backtracker. On the centre-clue grid it reaches depth 207 (385 matched edges, canonically rescored) in 60 s. Optimises depth, not score.
engines.collapse: Adding the study's three corner pins collapses its fixed scan path to depth 21 (canonical score 13), confirmed on 2 pinned variants: the scan never reaches a corner early, so a pinned corner dead-ends it at once.

engines.name: blackwood-cs
engines.note: Break-DFS (net8.0 retarget, one thread; search unchanged). On the centre-clue grid it reaches depth 119 (214 matched edges) in 60 s.
engines.collapse: It hardcodes its piece set and scan, so it cannot express the study's arbitrary corner pins; the matching constrained test is the five official clues, where its heuristic phase thrashes to depth 47 (canonical score 75) in 60 s.

rows.name: break-2
rows.note: The DFS study's strongest break-family engine, as the from-scratch baseline the community engines are measured against on the same grid.

rows.name: verhaard-reimpl
rows.note: Our comb-order reimplementation of the Verhaard search (the original binary is Win32-only). Runs on the JSON instance directly.

rows.name: mcgavin-c
rows.note: Its codegen backtracker specialises to the hint set; with only the centre clue it fills deep. Optimises depth, not matched-edge score.

rows.name: blackwood-cs
rows.note: Break-DFS. Reaches its plateau inside 60 s on a single centre clue; run to run it lands on the same depth.
